key_matchups: Sami Khedira vs Toni Kroos (Khedira’s half-volley hits Kroos in the back)
Oscar vs Germany’s corner clearance sequence (Oscar takes the corner, headed clear)
Bernard/Luis Gustavo/Marcelo vs Germany defending in box edge (Marcelo shoots from outside the area after a break)
Hulk/Bernard vs Germany’s clearing action (Hulk tries to square for Bernard; Germany clear)
Thomas Muller vs Fernandinho (Muller gives the ball away; Fernandinho clears)
Sami Khedira vs Thomas Muller (Khedira wins a corner; Muller is unmarked to score)
phases.summary: The opening minutes are unsettled and open, with both sides having early attacking moments rather than settling into a structured, attritional pattern. Germany first asserts sustained possession down the right, and within the phase they score from a corner: Khedira wins it and Muller arrives completely unmarked at the edge of the six-yard box to volley in low and hard, while Brazil’s defending is described as “shocking.”

key_matchups: Germany vs Brazil in transition through the space in behind Marcelo (highlighted as the repeated exploitation in the build-up to goal No2).
Philipp Lahm vs Marcelo (Lahm dispossesses Marcelo with a perfectly timed challenge to win a corner).
David Luiz vs Hulk on the left (David Luiz repeatedly plays long diagonals toward Hulk; Germany clear, then the ball comes back to David Luiz and he threads it again inside left toward Hulk).
Howedes vs Oscar (Howedes sticks a toe to rob Maicon’s cross/pick to Oscar).
Benedikt Howedes (defensive toe-poke in the sequence where Oscar receives after the switch to the right).
Kroos vs Bernard (Kroos wins a free-kick near the left touchline; Bernard is cited as the culprit).
Marcelo (subject of Lahm’s tackle and also involved in the winning of a corner after going to ground).
phases.summary: After early momentum swings, Germany take control of tempo and repeatedly create counter-attacking chances by exploiting space in behind Marcelo. The scoring run between 22' and 29' (Klose, Muller, Kroos, then Khedira) reflects that sustained dominance as Brazil struggle to prevent Germany’s ball-winning and fast access to attacking areas.

adjustments: Germany: switched personnel by replacing Mats Hummels with Per Mertesacker (formation/structure not specified in the text).

Brazil at half-time/trailing 5-0: halftime start includes double substitution—Hulk off for Ramires and Fernandinho off for Paulinho (which implies a change in midfield/attack balance, though exact shape is not stated).
key_matchups: David Luiz vs Klose (David Luiz elbow on Klose; mentioned as a moment that should have led to a sending off).
Marcelo’s defending vs Germany’s attacking play (Marcelo “caught out of position time and again”).
Maicon and Dante vs Germany’s attacking/breakdowns (both described as missing tackles and lunging carelessly).
phases.summary: By half-time, Germany are winning overwhelmingly (5-0) largely because they pass and move through Brazil with ease, while Brazil’s defending has collapsed across multiple areas. The half-time brief focuses on Brazil’s defensive disarray—David Luiz in particular is singled out for a disastrous performance (including an elbow incident on Klose), with Marcelo, Maicon, and Dante all underperforming—leading to a humiliating interval and setting up major personnel changes at the restart.

adjustments: Germany build attacks primarily down the left-hand side through the combination of Muller and Ozil, with frequent left-flank crossing/corners.
key_matchups: Paulinho vs Manuel Neuer (Neuer made a save after Paulinho’s right-footed drive; then a double-save from Paulinho’s follow-up after a through ball from Oscar).
Oscar vs Manuel Neuer (Oscar’s dinked through ball played Paulinho in; earlier Oscar had a penalty claim turned down).
Bernard vs Philipp Lahm/Lahm’s flank role (Bernard turns Lahm inside-out on the left and wins a corner off Muller).
Muller vs Julio Cesar (Muller waltzes through and Julio Cesar saves once he advances into range; Cesar also cuts out a left-flank cross at 56').
phases.summary: In this phase Brazil continue to surge forward for chances, creating several efforts that draw top saves from Neuer—particularly in sequences involving Oscar’s through balls and Paulinho’s finishes. Germany respond by repeatedly carving Brazil open through the left side, with Muller and Ozil combining, forcing corners and eventually adding a late goal after Schurrle comes on.

adjustments: Germany made a late substitution at 76': Sami Khedira off, Julian Draxler on.
Germany continued to press/pass-and-move into Brazil’s half late on (83'), with Brazil eventually “had enough.”
key_matchups: David Luiz vs Thomas Muller (David Luiz mis-kicks and then points/shouts at Muller standing nearby).
David Luiz vs Germany’s attacking actions: Luiz is described as being at fault for another goal at 78'.
Julio Cesar vs Schurrle’s through-ball chase (88': Julio Cesar off his line before Schurrle can latch on; David Luiz helps shepherd it back).
Manuel Neuer vs Brazil’s late chance: Neuer and defenders are described as furious with each other after Brazil’s late goal (90'), implying late defensive/communication issues for Germany.
phases.summary: From 75-90+, Germany turned the screw with relentless pass-and-move sorties into Brazil’s half, refusing to ease off even as the scoreline widened. Brazil’s resistance effectively broke down, with late errors centered on David Luiz leading directly to the 78' goal and then additional late damage culminating in Brazil scoring at 90' (1-7) while Germany were left furious with internal defensive coordination.
